page.content: The table presents critical data on various chemicals, detailing their abbreviations, units, sample sizes, means, standard deviations, minimums, maximums, and coefficients of variation. For Lignin (Ln), the unit is %, with a sample size of 20, a mean of 4.03, a standard deviation of 0.33, a minimum of 3.47, a maximum of 4.54, and a coefficient of variation (CV) of 0.082. Protein (Pt), also measured in %, has a sample size of 90, mean 8.41, standard deviation 1.02, minimum 6.2, maximum 12, and CV 0.121. Ash (Ash) is similarly assessed with a sample size of 90, mean 6.35, standard deviation 1.06, minimum 4.3, maximum 11, and CV 0.167. Moisture at 135 °C (Mt) shows a sample size of 90, mean 6.13, standard deviation 0.23, minimum 5.7, maximum 6.8, and CV 0.038. Neutral Detergent Fiber (NDF) has a sample size of 90, mean 65.47, standard deviation 2.11, minimum 57, maximum 70.7, and CV 0.032. Acid Detergent Fiber (ADF) lists a sample size of 90, mean 35.49, standard deviation 1.5, minimum 30.1, maximum 39.4, and CV 0.042. Total Digestible Nutrients (TDN) features a sample size of 90, mean 54.67, standard deviation 1.58, minimum 50.69, maximum 59.83, and CV 0.029.

page.content: Digestible Energy (DE) has a sample size of 90, mean 2.39, standard deviation 0.07, minimum 2.2, maximum 2.63, and CV 0.029. Net Energy for Lactation (NEI) reports a sample size of 90, mean 1.23, standard deviation 0.04, minimum 1.13, maximum 1.36, and CV 0.033. Net Energy for Maintenance (NEm) includes a sample size of 90, mean 1.16, standard deviation 0.05, minimum 1.01, maximum 0.49, and CV 0.043. Lastly, Net Energy for Gain (NEg) has a sample size of 90, mean 0.63, standard deviation 0.05, minimum 0.49, maximum 0.8, and CV 0.079.
